rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LEOLA J. WASHINGTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
rulings.Rulings: Granted per written order; however, distribution to the Leona Salter estate may not be made until Letters certified within 60 days have been filed for Kenneth W. Salter (or whoever is serving as that estate's personal representative). The next accounting must clearly show that this estate has only paid costs attributable to its fractional interests in the Louisiana properties. The request for the special administrator's and his attorney's fees is not a claim that would require allowance or rejection in any event, as fees for estate administration are not a "creditor's claim." The will was admitted on 10/2/19. Executor is holding cash well in excess of the FDIC insured amount in one bank. Status of filing current certified Letters for the Leona Salter estate will be set for 2/3/25. A petition for final distribution or a further report of status must be filed at least two weeks before 1/5/26. Appearance will be required on 1/5/26 if no petition is timely filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAUREEN E. CROWE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Granted per written order The Court opts to waive the following defects in order for the estate to close: (1) notice was mailed on 11/27/24 before this second amended petition was e-filed on 12/2/24 [LRSF 14.14 A.]; (2) an amended pleading completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes [CRC 7.3 (3)], so references to information in a prior pleading is meaningless (e.g., the statutory fee calculation should have been shown in this fully amended petition, among other things, and not as "calculated in the prior Probate Examiner notices," which are not part of the filed record in any event); (3) this is actually a petition for final distribution, as only a reserve is being requested to be withheld, the net amount of which a personal representative ordinarily requests to distribute without further court order (otherwise, Executor would have to file a petition for FINAL distribution); (4) not all of the disclosures required by CRC 7.550(b) have been made; (5) the specific assets are still not set forth, as required by CRC 7.651(b)(4) (the value of securities changes, so the number of shares of each holding is required to be set forth); (6) cash is cash, so it does not matter in which account or bank it is held; and (7) LRSF 14.35 I.2. requires that the DATE be set forth on which notice was given to the FTB.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee David Keeeler On Vacancy; For Waiver Of Bond
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Notices were given to the parties only, not to their attorneys. CRC 7.51.(b). If Paul Perdue is still the acting attorney in fact for Mabel Der (Alexander), notice to be given to the attorney in fact, too, if the instrument instructs so. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF QUMRUN N. MONIE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order Determining The Sale Of Real Property Is Necessary To Pay Debts, Devisees, And Expenses Of Administration And Is To The Advantage Of The Estate And In The Best Interest F The Interested Persons
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/29/2025. Each occupant of the property including Mohammed Rukshad Monie must be served in the manner required by Probate Code section 851(a)(2) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. applicable to service of a civil summons, absent a waiver of formal service signed by each occupant. Mere mailing is not an authorized manner of service under the applicable CCP provisions. Also note: Neither the amended petition nor the proposed order included a legal description of the subject real property.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF ISAAC STRAUS HAYMAKER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/13/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Immediately upon the occurrence of facts making it necessary or appropriate to increase the amount of the bond, the personal representative, or the guardian or conservator of the estate, must make an ex parte application for an order increasing the bond. [CRC 7.204.] An accounting cannot be approved until bond is sufficient. Bond should be rounded up to the nearest $1,000. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The assets on hand at the beginning of the accounting were not set forth as in the I&A, making the accounting review onerous; it appears that funds reported separately in the I&A were combined in the accounting. It would have been helpful if the assets on hand at the beginning either listed the assets as shown in the I&A or showed the I&A numbered entries under the total for each combined fund. (Receipts (dividends), however, still distinguished the separate funds.))

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THEODORE PAUL JONES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/13/2025, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (last updated and e-mailed to counsel on 10/16/2024): 1) No original will has been filed in this case. The case number assigned to the will previously deposited (starting with PDW) should be provided to the clerk when an amended petition for probate of the will is submitted. 2) An amended petition must be filed, and new notice (including new publication) must then be given, to address the following issues: i) The petition caption: If the decedent died testate, the first box (probate of will and for letters testamentary) must be checked. ii) There was no Attachment 3f(2) (copy of will). iii) Please provide the name and date of death of the decedent's predeceased spouse. iv) How is the petitioner related to the decedent? v) How is Cynthia Cochrane related to the decedent (and to the petitioner)? vi) A declaration required by CRC 7.52(a) must be filed to describe efforts to identify and locate relatives of the decedent and his predeceased spouse. A family tree showing the names, relationships, and dates of death of all known or reasonably ascertainable relatives (who would have been heirs entitled to notice) must be provided. vii) If the decedent was survived by no next of kin, notice must be given to the Attorney General, as the state would be the heir. Note: An appearance may be required on 01/13/2025 for the Court to consider whether to take this petition off calendar for lack of diligence, unless papers showing satisfactory progress to address the issues are filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAMESH CHANDER M. DASS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/3/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of publication of the notice has been filed. (2) If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Sec. 8113.] (3) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) The petitioner (the person named in item 2 of the petition) is missing from item 8 of the petition. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 1/20/25. (A supplement, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, must be filed to supplement his petition.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
